title: Caladenia interanea
taxonomy: Caladenia interanea is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid native to Australia. The species name interanea is derived from the Latin word for 'between', referring to the location of the plant. Caladenia interanea is a member of the genus Caladenia, commonly known as spider orchids. This species is part of the subtribe Caladeniinae, which consists of terrestrial orchids with a distinctive habit and flower structure.
uses: Caladenia interanea, also known as the "Fringed Petal Orchid," has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of chronic diseases.
